content:
3.2 Self-Evolving OS Loop

The OS evolves by applying the same procedural stages to itself.

Seed OS Waves: define upcoming macro-waves for the entire OS in VISION-META-SPEC documents.
Expand OS Components: when new needs arise (for example, a new archive type), expand SYSTEM and WORKFLOW specs rather than improvising.
Simulate OS Usage: periodically run “meta-sessions” where an AI inspects the archive and repositories, simulates future use, and proposes refactors or new modules.
Archive Governance Deltas: write governance changes and rationale into $ARCHIVE_ROOT/specs with CONTINUITY-layer indices.
Emit OS-Upgrade Prompts: store prompts that instruct future AIs on how to implement or further refine proposed changes.

This loop ensures that the OS is not static documentation but a living pattern that can be revised by its own procedures.

3.3 AI Roles and Read/Write Responsibilities

Architect can read and write $OS_ROOT/config and $OS_ROOT/specs, propose structural changes, and emit VISION and SYSTEM artifacts.
